skill.name: audit
skill.tagline: Cross-check completed work with a parallel panel of external models.
skill.shortSummary: Dispatch a self-contained audit brief to Codex and Kimi in parallel, collect file-backed reports, and compile a digest of agreements and disagreements for the user to rule on.
skill.fullSummary: The same audit brief goes to Codex and Kimi — two external agent CLIs with different biases — in one parallel dispatch. Their verbatim reports land on disk, then a compiled digest surfaces agreements as strong signals, splits as judgment calls, and every cited finding carries its report's path. The user rules before any finding is acted on.

highlights.title: Diverse-Model Panel
highlights.description: Codex and Kimi review the same work in parallel. Each model misses different things, so findings they agree on are strong signals while splits mark judgment calls worth your attention.

highlights.title: One Identical Brief
highlights.description: A single self-contained brief — with material inlined or readable paths cited — goes to every auditor. Identical input is what makes agreement and disagreement meaningful.

highlights.title: File-Backed Reports
highlights.description: Each auditor's report is written verbatim to the audit directory, and every cited finding in the digest carries its report's file path — a backtrace you can reopen at any time.

highlights.title: User Rules, Agent Waits
highlights.description: The digest presents findings and your assessment of the unique ones, but no finding is acted on until you rule. Disagreements are flagged for your judgment, never silently dropped.

steps.name: Load Dispatch Interface
steps.description: Invoke the use-external-agents skill for the router and brief contract.
steps.details: This skill builds on the external-agents router, the task brief contract, and the matrix call that reports which panel agents are installed and usable right now.

steps.name: Scope The Audit
steps.description: Name the audit and state what is being audited and the questions.
steps.details: Pick a kebab-case name; describe the diff, document, or decision under review; and list the specific questions the auditors must answer.

steps.name: Compose The Brief
steps.description: Write one self-contained brief tagged for the codex-kimi panel.
steps.details: Inline the material or point at readable paths, set TAGS: auditing and AGENTS: codex kimi, and require each auditor's full report with claim, evidence, and severity per finding.

steps.name: Dispatch And Persist
steps.description: Send the brief once; write each verbatim report to the audit directory.
steps.details: One router call launches the panel in parallel and returns each report; write them verbatim to ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dispatch-audit/<name>/<agent>.md before compiling.

steps.name: Compile And Wait For Ruling
steps.description: Report agreements, unique findings, and disagreements; then stop.
steps.details: Surface agreements first, unique findings with your assessment, and splits flagged for the user's judgment — every finding carries its report path. Act only after the user rules.
